narrative: The facility self-identified as a Small Quantity Generator but has been generating over 1,400 kg/month of D001 ignitable waste for the past four reporting periods, placing it well into LQG territory with no notification to the state. Drums in the central pad were observed with no accumulation start date written on the label and several were over 140 days old, exceeding the 90-day LQG limit. A 2,500-gal tank of corrosive D002 waste sat directly on bare asphalt with no secondary containment dike. Acid drums were stored on the same pallet as a cyanide-bearing plating sludge in clear incompatible-waste violation, and weekly inspection logs showed a six-week gap. Two outbound shipments to a Texas TSDF were sent without the required LDR notification attached to the manifest, and the signing employee held no current DOT hazmat training. The contingency plan had never been shared with the local fire department or LEPC.
